title: PLA Southern Theater Command Publishes Combat Readiness Patrol Video Near Scarborough Shoal
detail: China's People's Liberation Army Southern Theater Command released video footage on March 30 of 'combat readiness patrols' in the South China Sea near Scarborough Shoal (Huangyan Dao), featuring at least two Type 054A guided-missile frigates and J-15 carrier-based fighter jets. The PLA described the mission as 'tracking, monitoring, warnings, and expulsions,' and characterized the patrols as a direct response to Philippine 'provocations' following the BRP Benguet near-collision incident on March 25 and the Philippines-China BCM/FMC talks in Quanzhou. The deployment of carrier-based J-15 fighters alongside surface combatants near Scarborough represented an escalatory signal; the US issued no immediate public response but closely monitored PLAN movements via P-8A patrol aircraft.

title: AFP Reports 90 Unauthorized Chinese Vessels in West Philippine Sea During March 2026
detail: The Armed Forces of the Philippines (AFP) Western Command released its monthly maritime monitoring tally for March 2026, confirming that 90 unauthorized Chinese vessels were spotted within Philippine maritime zones during the month. The AFP's comprehensive count for March logged 22,755 total vessels in Philippine maritime zones — 19,328 foreign-flagged and 3,428 domestic — of which the 90 unauthorized Chinese vessels were flagged as a concern. The monthly tally comes amid the BRP Benguet incident (March 25), an active PLAN warship presence in the Spratlys, and continued negotiations following the Quanzhou BCM/FMC talks. The data highlighted the persistent pressure of Chinese maritime activity across multiple Philippine maritime zones simultaneously, including near Pag-asa Island, Second Thomas Shoal, and Scarborough Shoal.

sources.name: Armed Forces of the Philippines (AFP) WESCOM
